danger_signals.visual_form: Opponent's health bar reducing across top of screen
danger_signals.meaning: Imminent defeat; player approaching loss state

danger_signals.visual_form: Glowing Ki gauge building on character
danger_signals.meaning: Opponent preparing special attack; danger incoming

danger_signals.visual_form: Opponent moving into close range on split-screen

safety_signals.visual_form: Character positioned far from opponent on split-screen radar
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zone; time to charge Ki or plan strategy

safety_signals.visual_form: Bright, full health indicator at match start

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Playable as protagonists; Goku, Goten, Trunks, Gohan, Supreme Kai roster presence indicates heroic alignment
Majin Buu, Dabura, Majin Vegeta featured as playable antagonist variants

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Post-Cell saga, Majin Buu arc timeline
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Character roster reflects Majin Buu saga (Android 18, Majin Vegeta, Majin Buu, Dabura presence)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Younger generation fighters (Goten, Trunks) available alongside veterans; power scaling represented through character selection

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: One-on-one match structure; split-screen arena design keeping both fighters visible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Versus and tournament modes available; radar system shows spatial positioning within arena

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Split-screen display with radar tracking fighter positions; health bars prominent at top
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Characters perform dash movement (L/R button triggers); Ki charging mechanic visualized as glowing buildup

environmental_storytelling.example: Large Dabura sprite demonstrates technical animation advancement; sprite detail communicates character importance and power level
Presence of both Trunks variants (future and younger forms) visually indicates timeline branching and character age states
Radar system and split viewport communicate arena scale and fighter separation distance; environmental persistence shows bounded combat space

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Static on-screen pending player input; health bar full and ready
Dash toward player, perform meteor combo sequences, charge Ki for special attacks
Health bar depletes visually; character sprite may show knockback animation
Glowing visual effect on character; Y/B button held causes accumulation; enables Ki blast counterattack when released

notes: Dragon Ball Z: Super Butōden 3 is a 1-v-1 fighting game with limited environmental storytelling compared to narrative-driven titles. World-state communication occurs primarily through character roster selection (reflecting Majin Buu arc timeline), character transformations (implied through playable variants), and tournament structure. The game lacks a dedicated story mode (removed from Super Butōden 2), limiting narrative manifestation. Visual communication relies heavily on health bars, Ki indicators, and sprite-based character animation to convey combat state and danger. Faction identity is implicit through character selection (heroes vs. villains available as playable options) rather than explicit environmental markers. The split-screen feature with radar system communicates spatial world-state (arena geography, fighter distance). Character animation improvements noted in development (Dabura sprite complexity) suggest power and importance hierarchy through visual detail.
